Editor's Pick

Justice delayed: Acknowledging women pioneers in science

HeLa to a new world where progress in science matches dignity for patient

The omission of Henrietta Lacks, who donated the immortal cells that have facilitated biomedical research, from scientific history is a rare wrong that has been righted, recently. Thanks to the battle her family fought, today, more regulations will govern ethical research.
Ramya Kannan

Vasudevan Mukunth
